## 13. Custom code that v2.8 can now replace (reduce the fork surface)

Mapping our custom capabilities against v2.8's native features. Goal: drop custom code we no
longer need to maintain. **Validate each on staging during the re-fork** — native features may not
cover every nuance of our rules.

### ✅ Full replacement — delete custom, use core
| Custom code | v2.8 native replacement | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `contact-auto-scope` (listener) | a native **Workflow** (person created → set `scope`) | Listener is dead (unregistered); rebuild as a workflow. NOT contact-creation-related. |
| ~~`outlook-ingest` / `email-contact-sync`~~ | — | **CORRECTION (see §14a):** these are **LIVE cron jobs**, not stubs — do **not** delete. Native Microsoft driver / `contact-creation-manager` are *future* consolidations to evaluate, coupled to the router. |
| `program-stage-change`, `lat-stage-follow-up`, `new-program-tasks` (record→task listeners) | **Workflows** (record-update trigger → create-record/task actions, code & HTTP actions) | These are exactly what no-code Workflows do. Rebuild as workflows; delete the custom `@OnEvent` listeners. |
| Filter dropdown "load-more-on-scroll" (half of `ObjectFilterDropdownRecordSelect`) | v2.8 reworked the filter dropdown (+ **one-hop relation filters**, relation traversal) | Drop the scroll half; keep only the primaryBuyer/company-scoping business logic (see §9 E3). |

### 🟡 Partial — adopt core for the heavy lifting, keep a thin custom layer
| Custom code | v2.8 native | Keep what |
|---|---|---|
| `logic-functions/fireflies-ingest` | **`twenty-apps/internal/twenty-fireflies`** (native Fireflies app: transcript + summary on calendar events + workflow actions) | Use the native app for ingestion; keep only our meeting-note → company/department **routing** (a thin listener or a workflow). |
| `opportunity-summary.service.ts` + the `aiModelConfig` custom object | **AI Chat / agents** + models.dev model catalog | Drop the custom model-config object; express the running summary as a native agent/prompt. |
| `LookupValuesPage` | Native **select-field option management** in Settings (if that is what it administers) | Verify scope before dropping. |
| `MondayMorningDashboard` custom page | Native **Dashboard** object + page-layout widgets | Optional — the native React widget works fine; a native dashboard would be lower-maintenance. |

### 🔴 No native equivalent — keep (this is the real IP)
| Custom code | Why it stays |
|---|---|
| `email-router.service.ts` / `email-rerouter` / `so-extractor.service.ts` | Inbound email → **customer-company routing** by domain / PO# / subject / thread-scan. Unique domain logic; no core equivalent. |
| `clickup-sync` | No native ClickUp. Could move to a no-code **Workflow HTTP action** eventually, but no drop-in. |
| `DomainManagerPage` | Routing-config admin UI specific to our router. |
| Custom **fields** on company/person/opportunity + the 8 custom **objects** | Core has no equivalent; these are the data model. |

**Net (corrected — see §14a):** the real easy win is moving the **4 dead automation listeners**
(`program-stage-change`, `lat-stage-follow-up`, `new-program-tasks`, `contact-auto-scope`) to
no-code Workflows (they don't work today). The `outlook-ingest` / `email-contact-sync` /
`clickup-sync` **cron jobs are LIVE and stay** (only their unused placeholder service files were
deleted). Fireflies and the AI summary can shed custom weight onto the native app/agent over time.
The email router + outlook ingestion are the IP and stay.

## 14. Deep-dive: contact-creation verification + Workflow mapping

### 14a. ⚠️ CORRECTION: outlook-ingest / email-contact-sync / clickup-sync are LIVE, not stubs
**An earlier draft of §13–§15 wrongly called these three "dead stubs." They are live.** The
mistake: each name has TWO files — an unused placeholder `logic-functions/<name>/services/<name>.service.ts`
(which *was* a "In a real implementation…" stub) **and** the real implementation in
`crons/jobs/<name>.cron.job.ts` (registered in `pop-creations.module.ts`, actively running). The
placeholder dirs were unreferenced dead code and have been **deleted**; the cron jobs are untouched.

| Cron job | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| `outlook-ingest.cron.job` | ✅ **LIVE & CRITICAL** | polls Microsoft Graph every 15 min; **100% of the 10,507 `_emailMessage` rows have `outlookMessageId`** (all sourced here), latest `receivedAt` = today. **This is the email-ingestion pipeline** — it also calls the router + SO/PO extractor + AI summary on each email. |
| `email-contact-sync.cron.job` | ✅ **LIVE** | daily 2am; groups external email domains → finds/creates Company + Person (subdomain matching aligned with the router, noise-domain filtering). |
| `clickup-sync.cron.job` | ✅ **LIVE (idle)** | daily 7am; real ClickUp API sync — but **0 opportunities exist**, so nothing to act on. |

**Implication:** these are NOT delete candidates. `outlook-ingest` is tightly coupled to the router
(it writes the custom `emailMessage` object that the router classifies), so swapping to the native
Microsoft driver is a re-architecture, not a drop-in — keep it. Only the unused placeholder service
files were removed.

### 14b. Contact-creation: native is a *partial* overlap, not a drop-in
The **`email-contact-sync.cron.job`** is real (creates companies/people by email domain). v2.8's
`CreateCompanyAndPersonService` does similar from native messaging, but our version operates on the
custom `emailMessage` object and shares domain/subdomain logic with the router. So it is coupled to
our pipeline — treat native contact-creation as a *future* consolidation to evaluate at re-fork, not
a zero-risk drop. (The earlier "custom was a stub / drop with zero risk" claim was wrong.)

### 14c. Stage/automation listeners → native Workflows (DATABASE_EVENT trigger + actions)
v2.8 Workflows support `DATABASE_EVENT` (record created/updated/deleted), `CRON`, `WEBHOOK`,
`MANUAL` triggers and `CREATE_RECORD` / `UPDATE_RECORD` / `CODE` / `AI_AGENT` / `SEND_EMAIL` /
`HTTP_REQUEST` actions. All four custom `@OnEvent` listeners map cleanly:

| Custom listener | Native Workflow trigger | Filter | Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| `program-stage-change` | Record Updated · `opportunity` | `stage` ∈ {PROPOSAL, NEGOTIATION} | Create Record · `licensorApprovalThread` (linked to the opportunity) |
| `lat-stage-follow-up` | Record Updated · `licensorApprovalThread` | `stage` ∈ follow-up set | Create Record · `task` (linked to the LAT) |
| `new-program-tasks` | Record Created · `opportunity` | — | Create Record · `task` × 6 (the checklist templates), each linked |
| `contact-auto-scope` | Record Created · `person` | — | Update Record · set `scope` (DEPARTMENT / COMPANY_WIDE / IGNORED) — use a Code action for the branch, or 2 filtered workflows |

**Bonus — migrating fixes a latent bug.** `program-stage-change` and `lat-stage-follow-up` both do
`const { stage: newStage, stage: previousStage } = properties;` — destructuring the **same** field
twice, so their `if (newStage === previousStage) return;` guard can never be false and the action
**likely never fires today**. A native Workflow "updated → field changed to X" trigger removes the
bug entirely.

**Caveat — workflows are workspace data.** A no-code Workflow lives in the DB (like views), so to
keep it reproducible on re-fork it should be **defined in an app manifest** (the `twenty-fireflies`
app ships workflow-builder actions this way) rather than clicked together in the UI. Net trade:
~4 custom backend listeners (incl. a bug) → declarative workflow definitions in the POP Creations
app manifest.

### Summary of the fork-reduction opportunity (corrected)
- **Deleted (unreferenced placeholder dirs, zero loss):** the stub service files under
  `logic-functions/{outlook-ingest,email-contact-sync,clickup-sync}/`. The **cron jobs of the same
  names are LIVE and were kept.**
- **Genuinely dead — rebuild as native Workflows (post-v2.8, manifest-defined):**
  `program-stage-change`, `lat-stage-follow-up`, `new-program-tasks`, `contact-auto-scope` — 4
  unregistered listeners → declarative workflows (fixes the latent bug too).
- **Live — keep:** `outlook-ingest` (email pipeline), `email-contact-sync`, `clickup-sync` (idle),
  `email-router` / `email-rerouter` / `so-extractor`, `fireflies-ingest`, custom objects + fields.
- **Future consolidation to evaluate at re-fork (not drop-in):** Outlook ingest → native Microsoft
  driver; contact creation → `contact-creation-manager`; Fireflies → `twenty-fireflies`; AI summary
  → AI agent action — each is coupled to the custom email object/router, so validate before adopting.

## 15. VERIFIED: which custom features are silently dead in production (2026-06-02)

Verified by (1) code mechanism and (2) live production data in `workspace_93r34ew9zc9644a9y5f1yeylz`.

**Root cause for the automations:** the four standalone listeners subscribe with
`@OnEvent('opportunity.updated', …)` and read a **single-record** payload
(`payload.recordId`, `payload.properties.stage`). But the platform emits **batched** events
(`computeEventName` → `'opportunity.updated'`, payload = `WorkspaceEventBatch{ events: [...] }`,
each event having `.recordId` and `.properties.{before, after}`). The correct pattern —
`@OnDatabaseBatchEvent('opportunity', UPDATED)` with `payload.events[]` — is what the working main
listener and all native listeners use. So the four custom listeners fire but read `undefined`
fields off the batch (the stage ones then double-destructure `properties.stage`), crash into their
own try/catch, and **no-op**.

| Custom feature | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| `outlook-ingest.cron.job` | ✅ **LIVE & CRITICAL** | 10,507/10,507 emails have `outlookMessageId`; latest `receivedAt` = today — the whole email pipeline |
| `email-router` / `email-rerouter` | ✅ **WORKING** | 7,293 routed to a company (`COMPANY_ONLY`); 2,621 UNROUTED, 593 SKIPPED; `_emailMessage_reroute_backup` present |
| `email-contact-sync.cron.job` | ✅ **LIVE** | companies/people created from email domains (mostly since re-classified off `UNASSIGNED`) |
| `clickup-sync.cron.job` | ✅ **LIVE (idle)** | real ClickUp sync, but **0 opportunities** exist so nothing to do |
| `new-program-tasks` (listener) | ❌ **DEAD** | listener module not registered + 0 opportunities; 0 template tasks |
| `contact-auto-scope` (listener) | ❌ **DEAD** | module not registered; **all 8,602** people have `scope = NULL` |
| `program-stage-change` (listener) | ❌ **DEAD** | module not registered + wrong payload shape; 0 `_licensorApprovalThread` rows |
| `lat-stage-follow-up` (listener) | ❌ **DEAD** | module not registered + wrong payload shape; no LATs exist |

**Corrected count: the 4 `@OnEvent` automation *listeners* are dead** (their modules are never
imported, so they're never instantiated; the two stage ones also misread the batch payload). The
three `cron.job` features (`outlook-ingest`, `email-contact-sync`, `clickup-sync`) are **LIVE** —
the only "stubs" were unused placeholder service files (now deleted). `clickup-sync` and the
opportunity-triggered automations also have **0 opportunities** to act on.

**Implication for the re-fork:** keep the live cron jobs (especially `outlook-ingest`). Re-implement
the 4 dead automations as native Workflows (§14c) — they will work *for the first time*.

**⚠️ Context — the system is PRE-LAUNCH, not live.** The empty production data (0 opportunities,
0 LATs, all people unscoped) reflects that the CRM has **not gone live yet** and not everything
specced is exercised — it is **not** evidence of abandonment. The opportunity / program / LAT
features and their four automations are **intended functionality**. So the decision is settled:
**rebuild all four as native Workflows** (do not delete). They were specced, were never wired up
correctly, and have simply never had data flow through them yet.

**Decision (in the plan): rebuild all 4 automations as native, manifest-defined Workflows at the
re-fork** — `program-stage-change`, `lat-stage-follow-up`, `new-program-tasks`, `contact-auto-scope`.
The native Workflow engine already exists (even in v1.20) and is the correct mechanism; the custom
`@OnEvent` listeners were a reinvention that never ran. See Part 2 · Phase E-bis below.

payload.message: Two concrete corrections are emerging: the local checkout does not currently have a `v2.8.x` tag available, and the “manifest-defined Workflow” wording is not supported by the SDK surface I can see. That means the upgraded plan should replace certainty with a verification gate and a fallback implementation path, especially for the four automations.

content.text: Two concrete corrections are emerging: the local checkout does not currently have a `v2.8.x` tag available, and the “manifest-defined Workflow” wording is not supported by the SDK surface I can see. That means the upgraded plan should replace certainty with a verification gate and a fallback implementation path, especially for the four automations.

item.text:
# Revise `2.8_upgrade.md` Into a Safer Execution Plan

## Summary
Rewrite the upgrade plan so Part 2 reflects the later audit corrections in Parts 12-15. Keep the core strategy: re-fork from a verified upstream 2.8 target instead of merging. Change the plan’s tone from “copy module plus a few patches” to “preserve the business system first: custom schema, Outlook email ingest, router/rerouter, email-contact sync, views/nav, and POP UI.”

## Key Changes
- Update the headline and checklist to say the exact upstream target must be verified by `git fetch upstream --tags` and pinned to a commit SHA before work begins; do not assume local `v2.8.0` exists.
- Replace “drop in `pop-creations` wholesale = zero-conflict” with: copy the module, then adapt imports/internal APIs and separately reapply custom company/person/opportunity fields plus the `twenty-standard-application` metadata utilities.
- Make the business-critical keep list explicit: `outlook-ingest`, `email-router`, `email-rerouter`, `email-contact-sync`, `clickup-sync`, `so-extractor`, Fireflies routing, custom objects, custom fields, routing views, and POP navigation.
- Remove the unsupported certainty around “manifest-defined Workflows.” The revised plan should say:
  - first verify whether the target SDK actually supports defining full workflows;
  - if not, rebuild the four dead automations as reproducible app logic functions or registered upgrade-seeded workflow rows;
  - do not click workflows together manually as unreproducible workspace drift.
- Change POP SQL migration handling from “run all files with `psql`” to “convert or wrap POP migrations `001`-`012` as idempotent, committed upgrade steps; test them against a staging restore after Twenty’s native upgrade.”
- Expand the encryption section: keep `APP_SECRET`, verify the actual secret/encryption model in the target version, and test that Microsoft/Google connected accounts still decrypt and sync before production.
- Replace “deploy SERVER before FRONT” with a concrete deployment gate: identify whether server/front/worker are split images in Coolify; deploy server and worker first when split deployment is possible; otherwise deploy the single image and immediately run upgrade commands before opening user access.
- Add a restore drill to backups: create the dump, copy it off-host, run `pg_restore --list`, and restore it into staging before any production cutover.
- Update the frontend section to separate “business UI” from “nice-to-have fork polish.” Required: POP pages/widgets/routes, relation picker behavior, primary-buyer/company scoping, ParticipantChip. Optional/defer: IconPicker perf, generic admin workarounds, obsolete cherry-picked fixes.
- Add a final “do not do” list:
  - do not reapply footprint trimming;
  - do not hand-merge generated translations;
  - do not delete the `POP Creations CRM` app row unless its replacement is proven;
  - do not migrate dead v1.20 page-layout rows with SQL;
  - do not replace Outlook ingest/contact sync with native Twenty messaging as a drop-in.

## Concrete Document Edits
- Rewrite Part 2 Phase B to pin the target release by SHA and fail if the tag/head cannot be verified.
- Split current Phase C into:
  - Phase C1: port custom data model and standard-object field metadata;
  - Phase C2: port `pop-creations` backend and adapt it to v2.8 internals;
  - Phase C3: port live cron jobs and router services.
- Replace Phase E-bis with “Rebuild four dead automations reproducibly,” using this decision order: SDK workflow definition if proven, otherwise registered upgrade-seeded workflow DB rows, otherwise corrected `@OnDatabaseBatchEvent` logic functions.
- Replace Phase G3 with “run POP upgrade commands,” not raw `psql`, unless the command explicitly invokes those SQL files idempotently.
- Expand Phase H verification around deep email/contact behavior: Outlook import, display-name preservation, routed company assignment, rerouter, contact creation, ignore rules, subdomain matching, PO/SO extraction, routing views, and connected-account sync.
- Add a short “Future fork reduction” section: evaluate native Fireflies, native contact creation, native Microsoft messaging, and AI agents only after the v2.8 re-fork is stable; none are cutover blockers or drop-in replacements.

## Test Plan
- Static checks: server build, front build, SDK/app manifest build if used, and targeted tests for Gmail display-name parsing and router domain/subdomain matching.
- Migration checks: restore prod dump to staging, run Twenty native upgrades, run POP upgrade steps twice, verify second run is a no-op.
- Email/contact scenarios: ingest a Microsoft email from a known customer domain, route it to the expected company, create/update person/company via email-contact sync, reroute existing unrouted email, and verify ignore rules.
- UI scenarios: verify POP sidebar views, Monday dashboard, required POP widgets/routes, ParticipantChip right-click, primary-buyer/company scoping, and relation picker behavior.
- Cutover checks: server and worker healthy, no decryption errors, connected accounts sync, routing views present with no duplicates, old channel workspace objects removed without breaking email sync.

## Assumptions
- The near-term goal is a successful v2.8 re-fork that preserves POP business behavior, not a full app-platform migration.
- Deep email/contact behavior remains fork-owned unless a native replacement is proven in staging with the same routing semantics.
- The four dead automations are intended features and should be rebuilt, but they must be reproducible from code or committed upgrade steps.
- `company.creditStatus` remains intentionally dropped unless the user reverses that prior decision.

   345	(export via Coolify UI/API). Save as `backups/coolify-env-preupgrade.txt`. You will diff against
   346	this after adding `ENCRYPTION_KEY`.

   349	(`3a7298bf69` family), and take screenshots of the sidebar (folders + the 3 routing views) so you

   365	your new foundation. Everything below is re-applied **onto it**.

   370	```
   371	packages/twenty-server/src/modules/pop-creations/**

   374	contains the email router, the re-router cron, the 11 logic functions, AI opportunity summaries,
   375	and the SQL migrations `001`–`010`.

   384	server's root module exactly as in the old fork. **VERIFY:** in v2.8 the module-registration file
   385	may have moved; locate where feature modules are registered and add the import there.

   400	**Do NOT re-apply** the cherry-picked upstream fixes — they are native in v2.8: XSS href fix
   401	(#19282), `?token=` rejection (#20154), CalDAV Digest auth (#20135), SSRF hardening (#19963),
   402	email-composer rich-text (#19872), recurring Google Cal (#19748). Take v2.8's versions.

   407	
   408	Re-apply against v2.8's components (see Part 1 §9 for the full disposition). For each: open the
   409	old fork's diff for the file, understand the *intent*, and re-implement on the new file.
   410	
   411	| # | Feature | File(s) | Note |
   412	|---|---|---|---|

   418	| E6 | Lazy-load heavy UI assets | `…/ui/input/components/IconPicker.tsx` | v2.8 reworked IconPicker ~192 lines; your lazy-load may be moot — confirm perf need first |
   419	
   420	**Skip:** the 3 impersonation/admin-panel patches — v2.8 shipped a new admin panel that supersedes
   421	them. Take upstream and retest impersonation instead.
   422	
   423	**Gate:** `nx build twenty-front` passes; visually confirm each feature in a local/dev run.
   424	
   425	## 5-bis. Phase E-bis — Rebuild the 4 dead automations as native Workflows
   426	
   427	> **Context:** the system is **pre-launch**; these four were specced but never worked (custom
   428	> `@OnEvent` listeners that were never registered + misread the batched event payload — see §15).
   429	> **Do NOT port the listener code.** Build them as native, **manifest-defined** Workflows in the
   430	> POP Creations app so they are reproducible (not UI-clicked drift). The native Workflow engine
   431	> already exists; this is replacing dead custom code with no-code definitions.
   432	
   433	Build one workflow each (DATABASE_EVENT trigger → `create-record` / `update-record` action):
   434	
   435	| Workflow | Trigger | Filter | Action(s) |
   436	|---|---|---|---|
   437	| Auto-create LAT | `opportunity` **updated** | `stage` ∈ {PROPOSAL, NEGOTIATION} & changed | Create `licensorApprovalThread`, relate to the opportunity |
   438	| LAT follow-up task | `licensorApprovalThread` **updated** | `stage` ∈ follow-up set | Create `task`, relate to the LAT |
   439	| New-program checklist | `opportunity` **created** | — | Create 6 `task`s (the existing template titles), each related |
   440	| Contact auto-scope | `person` **created** | — | Set `scope` (use `if-else`/`filter` or a `code` action: has department → DEPARTMENT, else company → COMPANY_WIDE, else IGNORED) |
   441	
   442	Source-of-truth for the exact rules (stage lists, the 6 task titles, scope logic) is the existing
   443	listener code in `modules/pop-creations/logic-functions/{program-stage-change,lat-stage-follow-up,new-program-tasks,contact-auto-scope}/`
   444	— **keep those files until the workflows are built and verified, then delete them.**
   445	
   446	**Gate:** on staging, fire each trigger (move an opportunity to PROPOSAL, create a person, etc.) and
   447	confirm the records/tasks/scope are created — i.e. the features work **for the first time**.
   448	
   449	## 6. Phase F — Configuration & breaking changes (Coolify)
   450	
   451	Do these in **Coolify** (runtime config), not in Git.
   452	
   453	**Step F1 — `ENCRYPTION_KEY` (REQUIRED, breaking).** v2.x encrypts secrets at rest with a
   454	versioned envelope key. Generate and add it **before** the first v2.8 boot:
   455	```bash
   456	openssl rand -base64 32
   457	```
   458	Add as env var `ENCRYPTION_KEY` on app `rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92`. **VERIFY** the exact var name
   459	and format against v2.8 docs/`.env.example` — if it expects a versioned/JSON envelope, follow that
   460	shape. Without it, connected-account credentials cannot be decrypted and email sync breaks.
   461	
   462	**Step F2 — `APP_SECRET`.** No longer used for approved-domain validation / session cookies in
   463	v2.x. Keep it set (still used elsewhere) but do not rely on it for those paths.
   464	
   465	**Step F3 — `permissionFlag` → `rolePermissionFlag`.** Handled by an upgrade command (Phase G),
   466	not manual SQL. Just be aware roles get a backfill.
   467	
   468	**Gate:** Coolify env diff vs `backups/coolify-env-preupgrade.txt` shows `ENCRYPTION_KEY` added and
   469	nothing unintended removed.
   470	
   471	## 7. Phase G — Stage, build, run upgrades (on a NON-prod target first)
   472	
   473	> **Strongly recommended:** do this against a staging copy restored from the A1 dump. If no staging
   474	> exists, the same steps apply at cutover (Phase I) but with far less safety.
   475	
   476	**Step G1 — Push the branch; let GitHub Actions build the image.** The workflow graph is
   477	`lint → test → build/push image → deploy`. Confirm the image publishes to GHCR with an explicit,
   478	auditable tag (not a floating `latest` for staging).
   479	
   480	**Step G2 — Run Twenty's built-in version upgrade commands.** v2.x ships a versioned upgrade-command
   481	framework that walks each version's migrations, including:
   482	- `permissionFlag` → `rolePermissionFlag` rename + catalog backfill
   483	- **`upgrade:2-8:drop-channel-standard-objects`** — drops `messageChannel`/`messageFolder`/
   484	  `calendarChannel` from workspace schemas (they moved to core metadata). **This is the destructive
   485	  one.** It does *not* touch our routing code (we don't use those objects — Part 1 §5/§7).
   486	
   487	**VERIFY** the exact invocation in v2.8 (`package.json` scripts / docs). It is typically the
   488	server's command runner, e.g.:
   489	```bash
   490	# VERIFY exact script name in v2.8 before running:
   491	docker exec -i <server-container> yarn command:prod upgrade
   492	```
   493	
   494	**Step G3 — Apply our pop-creations SQL migrations in numeric order** (these are *our* migrations,
   495	separate from Twenty's framework):
   496	```bash
   497	for f in $(ls packages/twenty-server/src/modules/pop-creations/migrations/0*.sql | sort); do
   498	  echo ">> $f"
   499	  docker exec -i <postgres-container> psql -U twenty -d twenty < "$f"
   500	done
   501	```
   502	- `008` — `_emailMessage` routing index. **VERIFY** the `_emailMessage` table/column names still
   503	  exist under v2.8's schema before applying; adjust if renamed.
   504	- `010` — routing views + nav (idempotent; safe to re-run).
   505	
   506	**Gate (staging):** see Phase H. Do not promote to prod until every check passes.
   507	
   508	## 8. Phase H — Verification checklist (run on staging, repeat at cutover)
   509	
   510	1. **App boots** — server and worker containers healthy; no decryption errors in logs.
   511	2. **Connected accounts still sync** — trigger a manual sync; new emails import.
   512	3. **Email routing works** — send a test email from a known customer domain; confirm the router
   513	   assigns the right company/department (check the pop-creations router logs).
   514	4. **Re-router cron runs** — confirm it processes and finds improvements (uses the `008` index).
   515	5. **Routing views present & correct** — sidebar shows **Needs Routing** (Companies), **Unrouted
   516	   Emails** (Email), **Unrouted Notes** (Meeting Notes); each lists the expected records.
   517	6. **ParticipantChip** right-click opens the People record.
   518	7. **Channel objects gone from data model** but sync unaffected (expected after `drop-channel`).
   519	8. **Impersonation** works via the new v2.8 admin panel.
   520	9. **No duplicate folders/views** — re-running `010` is a no-op:
   521	   ```sql
   522	   SELECT name,count(*) FROM core."navigationMenuItem"
   523	   WHERE type='FOLDER' AND name IN ('Companies','Email','Meeting Notes')
   524	   GROUP BY name;   -- each must be exactly 1
   525	   ```
   526	
   527	## 9. Phase I — Production cutover
   528	
   529	**Step I1 — Fresh prod backup** (repeat A1; the staging run may be hours/days old).
   530	**Step I2 — Set `ENCRYPTION_KEY`** in prod Coolify (Phase F) if not already.
   531	**Step I3 — Deploy SERVER before FRONT.** Several v2.x changes require this ordering. Deploy the
   532	server image via the Coolify API job, wait for healthy, then the front.
   533	**Step I4 — Run Phase G2 (Twenty upgrades) then G3 (our migrations)** against prod.
   534	**Step I5 — Run the full Phase H checklist against `https://crm.designflow.app`.**
   535	**Step I6 — Verify BOTH `server-…` and `worker-…` containers** are on the new image and healthy.
   536	
   537	**Rollback:** if any gate fails — redeploy the previous image digest (recorded in A3) via Coolify,
   538	and if the destructive migration already ran, **restore the A1/I1 dump**:
   539	```bash
   540	docker exec -i twenty-postgres pg_restore -U twenty -d twenty --clean --if-exists \
   541	  < backups/twenty_preupgrade_YYYYMMDD.dump
   542	```
   543	(The channel-drop is not reversible forward without restore — that is why the dump is mandatory.)
   544	
   545	## 10. Phase J — Post-cutover
   546	
   547	1. **Fast-forward `main`** to the rebased branch (this becomes the new fork base).
   548	2. **Update memory + docs** — new base is `v2.8.0`; record the new merge-base for the *next* upgrade.
   549	3. **Confirm no leftover drift** — the routing views/folders now come from migration `010`; nothing
   550	   should exist in prod that isn't in the repo.
   551	4. **Monitor** sync + routing for 48h; watch worker logs for decryption or migration warnings.
   552	5. **Schedule the next upgrade sooner** — staying current from v2.8 is far cheaper than this jump
   553	   was from v1.20.
   554	
   555	## 11. Step count summary
   556	
   557	11 phases (incl. Phase E-bis), ~30+ discrete steps. The irreversible ones are exactly two — **`ENCRYPTION_KEY` must be
   558	set before first boot** (F1) and **the channel-drop migration** (G2) — both fully covered by the
   559	Phase A/I database dump. Everything else is build-and-verify with a redeploy rollback.
   560	
   561	---
   562	
   563	## 12. Production drift audit (2026-06-02)
   564	
   565	Goal: find everything that exists **only in production** (created via UI/API) and would be **lost
   566	on a re-fork** because it is not reproducible from the repo. Audited `core.objectMetadata`,
   567	`core.fieldMetadata`, `core.view`, `core.navigationMenuItem` against committed code + migrations.
   568	
   569	### Data model — ESSENTIALLY CLEAN ✅
   570	- **8 custom objects** (`aiModelConfig`, `department`, `emailMessage`, `factory`, `ignoreRule`,

-## 5-bis. Phase E-bis — Rebuild the 4 dead automations as native Workflows

+# Part 2 — Execution Plan (re-fork to verified v2.8 target)
+
+> **Audience:** a developer who has *never* touched this codebase. Follow the phases in order.
+> This is a business-system preservation plan first, not a generic upgrade. The core strategy is
+> still re-fork-and-reapply, but the target release, workflow support, encryption model, and
+> migration mechanics must be verified before production work begins.
+
+## 0. Facts and non-negotiables
+
+| Thing | Value |
+|---|---|
+| Production URL | `https://crm.designflow.app` |
+| VPS (inspection/SSH only — never deploy via SSH) | `178.156.180.212` |
+| Coolify app UUID | `rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92` |
+| Deployed image | `ghcr.io/u2giants/twenty:latest` |
+| Server container filter | `server-rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92` |
+| Worker container filter | `worker-rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92` |
+| Postgres container | `twenty-postgres` (`psql -U twenty -d twenty`) |
+| Workspace schema | `workspace_93r34ew9zc9644a9y5f1yeylz` |
+| Repo / upstream | `github.com/u2giants/twenty` / `github.com/twentyhq/twenty` |
+| Release pipeline | GitHub Actions -> GHCR image -> Coolify API deploy (**never** SSH, never `--no-verify`) |
+| Current base / target | `v1.20.0` + custom commits -> **verified v2.8 release commit**, pinned by SHA |
+
+**Business-critical behavior that must survive:**
+- Outlook email ingestion (`outlook-ingest.cron.job`) and the custom `emailMessage` pipeline.
+- `email-router`, `email-rerouter`, `so-extractor`, ignore rules, domain/subdomain matching, and PO/SO extraction.
+- Daily `email-contact-sync` company/person creation and its shared routing-domain semantics.
+- `clickup-sync`, Fireflies meeting-note routing, AI opportunity summary, and POP custom objects/fields.
+- Routing views/nav, POP navigation, Monday dashboard, required POP widgets/routes, relation picker behavior, primary-buyer/company scoping, and `ParticipantChip`.
+
+**Do not do:**
+- Do not reapply footprint trimming or bulk package/core deletions.
+- Do not hand-merge generated translation files; regenerate them.
+- Do not delete the `POP Creations CRM` app row unless a code-backed replacement is already proven.
+- Do not migrate the dead v1.20 page-layout rows with SQL; remount wanted dashboards in code or a verified v2.x app mechanism.
+- Do not replace Outlook ingest or email-contact sync with native Twenty messaging/contact creation as a drop-in.
+- Do not click together workflows in the UI as unreproducible workspace drift.
+
+## 1. Phase A — Safety net and restore drill
+
+**Step A1 — Full production database backup.**
+```bash
+STAMP="$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"
+docker exec -t twenty-postgres pg_dump -U twenty -d twenty -Fc \
+  -f "/tmp/twenty_preupgrade_${STAMP}.dump"
+docker cp "twenty-postgres:/tmp/twenty_preupgrade_${STAMP}.dump" "./backups/"
+pg_restore --list "./backups/twenty_preupgrade_${STAMP}.dump" >/tmp/twenty_restore_list.txt
+```
+**Verify:** dump exists, is > 0 bytes, `pg_restore --list` succeeds, and the dump is copied off the
+VPS. Restore this dump into a staging database before any production cutover.
+
+**Step A2 — Snapshot current runtime state.** Export Coolify env vars for app
+`rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92`, record the running image digest and current git SHA, and screenshot the
+sidebar folders/views. Save these artifacts outside the deployment host.
+
+**Gate:** do not proceed until the dump has been restored into staging and the restored staging app
+can boot on the current image.
+
+## 2. Phase B — Verify and pin the upstream target
+
+**Step B1 — Fetch and verify the exact target.**
+```bash
+git fetch upstream --tags --prune
+git tag -l 'v2.8*' --sort=-v:refname
+git rev-parse <verified-v2.8-tag-or-sha>
+git log -1 --format='%H %cI %s' <verified-v2.8-tag-or-sha>
+```
+If no v2.8 tag or intended upstream SHA can be verified locally after fetch, **stop**. Do not branch
+from a stale `upstream/main`, a missing tag, or an assumed release name.
+
+**Step B2 — Create the re-fork branch from the pinned SHA.**
+```bash
+git switch -c v28-refork <PINNED_UPSTREAM_SHA>
+git describe --tags --always
+```
+Do this in a separate clone or worktree if the server checkout blocks branch creation. Do not rewrite
+`main` until production cutover is complete and verified.
+
+**Gate:** record `<PINNED_UPSTREAM_SHA>` in this document before implementation begins.
+
+## 3. Phase C — Preserve the POP business system
+
+**Step C1 — Port the custom data model first.** Reapply all POP object/field definitions before
+runtime services:
+- The 8 POP custom objects from `modules/pop-creations/standard-objects/`.
+- Custom fields on standard `company`, `person`, and `opportunity` objects.
+- The matching `twenty-standard-application` field-metadata utilities, including the
+  `pop-creations/compute-*` utilities.
+- Keep `company.creditStatus` intentionally dropped unless that prior decision is reversed.
+
+This is the biggest backend reapply surface. It is not contained inside the clean `pop-creations`
+module, and v2.8 may have moved the standard-application metadata pipeline.
+
+**Step C2 — Port the `pop-creations` backend module and adapt it.** Copy
+`packages/twenty-server/src/modules/pop-creations/**`, then fix imports, module wiring, queue/job
+registration, workspace manager usage, event listener decorators, and internal repository APIs
+against the pinned v2.8 target. Treat "no Git conflicts" as only a copyability fact, not proof that
+the code compiles or runs.
+
+**Step C3 — Keep live cron jobs and router services.** Preserve and register:
+- `outlook-ingest.cron.job`, `email-contact-sync.cron.job`, and `clickup-sync.cron.job`.
+- `email-router.service`, `email-rerouter`, `so-extractor`, Fireflies routing, and opportunity summary.
+- Gmail display-name preservation in the shared messaging parser files.
+
+**Gate:** server builds, the POP module loads, and targeted tests or local harnesses cover custom
+field metadata, Gmail display-name parsing, router domain/subdomain matching, and rerouter queries.
+
+## 4. Phase D — Backend shared-file patches
+
+Reapply shared-file changes by intent against the v2.8 files. Do not paste old files over new ones.
+
+| Feature | Required action |
+|---|---|
+| Gmail display names | Preserve sender/recipient display names from Gmail headers in the v2.8 parser utilities. |
+| Messaging DI/module wiring | Add only the dependencies required by the display-name change and POP services. |
+| POP jobs/services | Register POP cron jobs, queue jobs, and services in the v2.8 server module structure. |
+| Cherry-picked upstream fixes | Do not reapply; take v2.8's XSS, CalDAV, email composer, calendar, and token-handling fixes. |
+
+**Gate:** `nx build twenty-server` passes and no POP service is silently unregistered.
+
+## 5. Phase E — Frontend: required business UI vs optional polish
+
+**Required business UI to port:**
+- POP pages/widgets/routes, including Monday dashboard and any code-mounted dashboard widgets chosen
+  to replace dead page-layout drift.
+- Relation picker behavior, department/company picker filtering, inline name/company population, and
+  primary-buyer/company scoping.
+- `ParticipantChip` right-click-to-open-People behavior.
+- POP navigation/view behavior that users rely on.
+
+**Optional/defer unless still needed after v2.8 retest:**
+- IconPicker lazy-load perf patch.
+- Generic object color coordination.
+- Admin/impersonation workarounds superseded by v2.8.
+- Filter-dropdown load-more behavior if v2.8 already fixed it.
+
+**Gate:** `nx build twenty-front` passes and the required business UI is visually verified in a
+local or staging run.
+
+## 6. Phase F — Rebuild the four dead automations reproducibly
+
+The four intended automations are pre-launch features that never worked in production. Rebuild them,
+but only with a reproducible code/upgrade path.
+
+Decision order:
+1. Verify whether the pinned v2.8 SDK/app system supports defining full workflows in code. If it
+   does, define the four workflows there.
+2. If not, create registered, idempotent upgrade steps that seed the required workflow rows and
+   versions from code.
+3. If workflow seeding is not practical, reimplement them as corrected app/server logic functions
+   using the v2.8 database-event payload shape, preferably `@OnDatabaseBatchEvent`.
+
+Do not use unreproducible UI-clicked workflows as the final implementation.
+
+| Automation | Trigger | Behavior |
+|---|---|---|
+| Auto-create LAT | `opportunity` updated; stage changed into PROPOSAL/NEGOTIATION | Create related `licensorApprovalThread`. |
+| LAT follow-up task | `licensorApprovalThread` updated into follow-up stage | Create related follow-up `task`. |
+| New-program checklist | `opportunity` created | Create the six template tasks from the existing listener source. |
+| Contact auto-scope | `person` created | Set scope from department/company presence, else ignored. |
+
+**Gate:** on staging, fire all four triggers and verify the created/updated records. Keep old listener
+source until the replacement passes, then delete or quarantine the dead code.
+
+## 7. Phase G — Configuration and encryption
+
+Do these in **Coolify** or the target runtime config, not in Git.
+
+**Step G1 — Verify the target encryption model.** Before adding new secrets, inspect the pinned
+target's config schema/docs and confirm whether the required variable is `ENCRYPTION_KEY`,
+`APP_SECRET`, a versioned key envelope, or another key-rotation mechanism. Keep the existing
+`APP_SECRET`; do not assume it is obsolete.
+
+**Step G2 — Add only confirmed required env vars.** Add the target-required encryption value before
+the first upgraded boot. Diff against the A2 env snapshot and confirm nothing else changed.
+
+**Step G3 — Connected-account decryptability check.** On staging, Microsoft/Google connected accounts
+must decrypt, sync, and send/receive as expected before production.
+
+**Gate:** no server/worker decryption errors; Outlook ingest and native connected-account sync both
+work on staging.
+
+## 8. Phase H — Build, stage, and run upgrades
+
+**Step H1 — Build an auditable image.** Push the branch, let GitHub Actions build, and deploy staging
+from an explicit GHCR tag or digest. Do not use floating `latest` for staging validation.
+
+**Step H2 — Run Twenty native upgrade commands.** Verify the exact command runner in the pinned
+target, then run the official version upgrade path. Confirm the channel-object migration command
+exists before naming/running it; if `upgrade:2-8:drop-channel-standard-objects` is present, treat it
+as destructive and restore-only reversible.
+
+**Step H3 — Run POP upgrade commands, not ad-hoc raw SQL.** Convert or wrap POP migrations `001`-`012`
+as committed, idempotent upgrade steps. If a wrapper intentionally executes SQL files, the command
+must enforce ordering, idempotency, logging, and repeatability. Run POP upgrades twice on staging and
+verify the second run is a no-op.
+
+**Gate:** staging has passed native upgrades, POP upgrades, and the full Phase I checklist.
+
+## 9. Phase I — Verification checklist
+
+Run on staging, then repeat at production cutover.
+
+1. Server and worker containers are healthy, on the expected image digest, and free of decryption or
+   migration errors.
+2. Microsoft connected accounts decrypt; Outlook ingest imports a test email into custom
+   `emailMessage`.
+3. Gmail parser test preserves display names.
+4. Email router assigns a known customer-domain email to the expected company/department.
+5. Subdomain matching, ignore rules, PO/SO extraction, and thread/subject scans behave as before.
+6. Rerouter cron runs and can improve previously unrouted emails.
+7. Email-contact sync creates/updates Company and Person records with the expected domain rules.
+8. Routing views/nav are present: **Needs Routing**, **Unrouted Emails**, **Unrouted Notes**, plus
+   captured grouping/panel views from migrations `010`/`011`.
+9. POP UI works: Monday dashboard, required POP widgets/routes, relation picker behavior,
+   primary-buyer/company scoping, and `ParticipantChip`.
+10. Channel workspace objects are gone only after the native migration, and email/calendar sync still
+    works.
+11. The four rebuilt automations fire successfully.
+12. Re-running POP upgrade steps creates no duplicate folders/views/workflows.
+
+## 10. Phase J — Production cutover
+
+**Step J1 — Fresh prod backup and restore check.** Repeat Phase A immediately before cutover.
+
+**Step J2 — Confirm deployment topology.** Identify whether Coolify deploys split server/front/worker
+images or a single image:
+- If split, deploy server and worker first, run upgrade commands, then open/deploy front.
+- If single image, deploy once, immediately run upgrade commands, and keep user access gated until
+  Phase I passes.
+
+**Step J3 — Run upgrades in order.** Run Twenty native upgrades, then POP upgrade commands. Never run
+undocumented SQL by hand.
+
+**Step J4 — Repeat Phase I against `https://crm.designflow.app`.**
+
+**Rollback:** if any gate fails, redeploy the previous image digest through Coolify. If destructive
+migrations already ran, restore the fresh cutover dump:
+```bash
+docker exec -i twenty-postgres pg_restore -U twenty -d twenty --clean --if-exists \
+  < backups/twenty_preupgrade_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.dump
+```
+
+## 11. Phase K — Post-cutover and future fork reduction
+
+1. Fast-forward `main` to the verified re-fork branch and record the pinned upstream SHA as the new base.
+2. Monitor worker/server logs, Outlook ingest, routing, rerouting, and contact sync for 48 hours.
+3. Confirm no remaining production drift affects correctness.
+4. Evaluate fork reduction only after the v2.8 re-fork is stable: native Fireflies, native contact
+   creation, native Microsoft messaging, AI agents, native dashboards, and workflow tooling. None are
+   cutover blockers and none are assumed drop-in replacements for POP routing semantics.
*** End Patch

+# Part 2 — Execution Plan (re-fork to verified v2.8 target)
+
+> **Audience:** a developer who has *never* touched this codebase. Follow the phases in order.
+> This is a business-system preservation plan first, not a generic upgrade. The core strategy is
+> still re-fork-and-reapply, but the target release, workflow support, encryption model, and
+> migration mechanics must be verified before production work begins.
+
+## 0. Facts and non-negotiables
+
+| Thing | Value |
+|---|---|
+| Production URL | `https://crm.designflow.app` |
+| VPS (inspection/SSH only — never deploy via SSH) | `178.156.180.212` |
+| Coolify app UUID | `rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92` |
+| Deployed image | `ghcr.io/u2giants/twenty:latest` |
+| Server container filter | `server-rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92` |
+| Worker container filter | `worker-rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92` |
+| Postgres container | `twenty-postgres` (`psql -U twenty -d twenty`) |
+| Workspace schema | `workspace_93r34ew9zc9644a9y5f1yeylz` |
+| Repo / upstream | `github.com/u2giants/twenty` / `github.com/twentyhq/twenty` |
+| Release pipeline | GitHub Actions -> GHCR image -> Coolify API deploy (**never** SSH, never `--no-verify`) |
+| Current base / target | `v1.20.0` + custom commits -> **verified v2.8 release commit**, pinned by SHA |
+
+**Business-critical behavior that must survive:**
+- Outlook email ingestion (`outlook-ingest.cron.job`) and the custom `emailMessage` pipeline.
+- `email-router`, `email-rerouter`, `so-extractor`, ignore rules, domain/subdomain matching, and PO/SO extraction.
+- Daily `email-contact-sync` company/person creation and its shared routing-domain semantics.
+- `clickup-sync`, Fireflies meeting-note routing, AI opportunity summary, and POP custom objects/fields.
+- Routing views/nav, POP navigation, Monday dashboard, required POP widgets/routes, relation picker behavior, primary-buyer/company scoping, and `ParticipantChip`.
+
+**Do not do:**
+- Do not reapply footprint trimming or bulk package/core deletions.
+- Do not hand-merge generated translation files; regenerate them.
+- Do not delete the `POP Creations CRM` app row unless a code-backed replacement is already proven.
+- Do not migrate the dead v1.20 page-layout rows with SQL; remount wanted dashboards in code or a verified v2.x app mechanism.
+- Do not replace Outlook ingest or email-contact sync with native Twenty messaging/contact creation as a drop-in.
+- Do not click together workflows in the UI as unreproducible workspace drift.
+
+## 1. Phase A — Safety net and restore drill
+
+**Step A1 — Full production database backup.**
+```bash
+STAMP="$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"
+docker exec -t twenty-postgres pg_dump -U twenty -d twenty -Fc \
+  -f "/tmp/twenty_preupgrade_${STAMP}.dump"
+docker cp "twenty-postgres:/tmp/twenty_preupgrade_${STAMP}.dump" "./backups/"
+pg_restore --list "./backups/twenty_preupgrade_${STAMP}.dump" >/tmp/twenty_restore_list.txt
+```
+**Verify:** dump exists, is > 0 bytes, `pg_restore --list` succeeds, and the dump is copied off the
+VPS. Restore this dump into a staging database before any production cutover.
+
+**Step A2 — Snapshot current runtime state.** Export Coolify env vars for app
+`rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92`, record the running image digest and current git SHA, and screenshot the
+sidebar folders/views. Save these artifacts outside the deployment host.
+
+**Gate:** do not proceed until the dump has been restored into staging and the restored staging app
+can boot on the current image.
+
+## 2. Phase B — Verify and pin the upstream target
+
+**Step B1 — Fetch and verify the exact target.**
+```bash
+git fetch upstream --tags --prune
+git tag -l 'v2.8*' --sort=-v:refname
+git rev-parse <verified-v2.8-tag-or-sha>
+git log -1 --format='%H %cI %s' <verified-v2.8-tag-or-sha>
+```
+If no v2.8 tag or intended upstream SHA can be verified locally after fetch, **stop**. Do not branch
+from a stale `upstream/main`, a missing tag, or an assumed release name.
+
+**Step B2 — Create the re-fork branch from the pinned SHA.**
+```bash
+git switch -c v28-refork <PINNED_UPSTREAM_SHA>
+git describe --tags --always
+```
+Do this in a separate clone or worktree if the server checkout blocks branch creation. Do not rewrite
+`main` until production cutover is complete and verified.
+
+**Gate:** record `<PINNED_UPSTREAM_SHA>` in this document before implementation begins.
+
+## 3. Phase C — Preserve the POP business system
+
+**Step C1 — Port the custom data model first.** Reapply all POP object/field definitions before
+runtime services:
+- The 8 POP custom objects from `modules/pop-creations/standard-objects/`.
+- Custom fields on standard `company`, `person`, and `opportunity` objects.
+- The matching `twenty-standard-application` field-metadata utilities, including the
+  `pop-creations/compute-*` utilities.
+- Keep `company.creditStatus` intentionally dropped unless that prior decision is reversed.
+
+This is the biggest backend reapply surface. It is not contained inside the clean `pop-creations`
+module, and v2.8 may have moved the standard-application metadata pipeline.
+
+**Step C2 — Port the `pop-creations` backend module and adapt it.** Copy
+`packages/twenty-server/src/modules/pop-creations/**`, then fix imports, module wiring, queue/job
+registration, workspace manager usage, event listener decorators, and internal repository APIs
+against the pinned v2.8 target. Treat "no Git conflicts" as only a copyability fact, not proof that
+the code compiles or runs.
+
+**Step C3 — Keep live cron jobs and router services.** Preserve and register:
+- `outlook-ingest.cron.job`, `email-contact-sync.cron.job`, and `clickup-sync.cron.job`.
+- `email-router.service`, `email-rerouter`, `so-extractor`, Fireflies routing, and opportunity summary.
+- Gmail display-name preservation in the shared messaging parser files.
+
+**Gate:** server builds, the POP module loads, and targeted tests or local harnesses cover custom
+field metadata, Gmail display-name parsing, router domain/subdomain matching, and rerouter queries.
+
+## 4. Phase D — Backend shared-file patches
+
+Reapply shared-file changes by intent against the v2.8 files. Do not paste old files over new ones.
+
+| Feature | Required action |
+|---|---|
+| Gmail display names | Preserve sender/recipient display names from Gmail headers in the v2.8 parser utilities. |
+| Messaging DI/module wiring | Add only the dependencies required by the display-name change and POP services. |
+| POP jobs/services | Register POP cron jobs, queue jobs, and services in the v2.8 server module structure. |
+| Cherry-picked upstream fixes | Do not reapply; take v2.8's XSS, CalDAV, email composer, calendar, and token-handling fixes. |
+
+**Gate:** `nx build twenty-server` passes and no POP service is silently unregistered.
+
+## 5. Phase E — Frontend: required business UI vs optional polish
+
+**Required business UI to port:**
+- POP pages/widgets/routes, including Monday dashboard and any code-mounted dashboard widgets chosen
+  to replace dead page-layout drift.
+- Relation picker behavior, department/company picker filtering, inline name/company population, and
+  primary-buyer/company scoping.
+- `ParticipantChip` right-click-to-open-People behavior.
+- POP navigation/view behavior that users rely on.
+
+**Optional/defer unless still needed after v2.8 retest:**
+- IconPicker lazy-load perf patch.
+- Generic object color coordination.
+- Admin/impersonation workarounds superseded by v2.8.
+- Filter-dropdown load-more behavior if v2.8 already fixed it.
+
+**Gate:** `nx build twenty-front` passes and the required business UI is visually verified in a
+local or staging run.
+
+## 6. Phase F — Rebuild the four dead automations reproducibly
+
+The four intended automations are pre-launch features that never worked in production. Rebuild them,
+but only with a reproducible code/upgrade path.
+
+Decision order:
+1. Verify whether the pinned v2.8 SDK/app system supports defining full workflows in code. If it
+   does, define the four workflows there.
+2. If not, create registered, idempotent upgrade steps that seed the required workflow rows and
+   versions from code.
+3. If workflow seeding is not practical, reimplement them as corrected app/server logic functions
+   using the v2.8 database-event payload shape, preferably `@OnDatabaseBatchEvent`.
+
+Do not use unreproducible UI-clicked workflows as the final implementation.
+
+| Automation | Trigger | Behavior |
+|---|---|---|
+| Auto-create LAT | `opportunity` updated; stage changed into PROPOSAL/NEGOTIATION | Create related `licensorApprovalThread`. |
+| LAT follow-up task | `licensorApprovalThread` updated into follow-up stage | Create related follow-up `task`. |
+| New-program checklist | `opportunity` created | Create the six template tasks from the existing listener source. |
+| Contact auto-scope | `person` created | Set scope from department/company presence, else ignored. |
+
+**Gate:** on staging, fire all four triggers and verify the created/updated records. Keep old listener
+source until the replacement passes, then delete or quarantine the dead code.
+
+## 7. Phase G — Configuration and encryption
+
+Do these in **Coolify** or the target runtime config, not in Git.
+
+**Step G1 — Verify the target encryption model.** Before adding new secrets, inspect the pinned
+target's config schema/docs and confirm whether the required variable is `ENCRYPTION_KEY`,
+`APP_SECRET`, a versioned key envelope, or another key-rotation mechanism. Keep the existing
+`APP_SECRET`; do not assume it is obsolete.
+
+**Step G2 — Add only confirmed required env vars.** Add the target-required encryption value before
+the first upgraded boot. Diff against the A2 env snapshot and confirm nothing else changed.
+
+**Step G3 — Connected-account decryptability check.** On staging, Microsoft/Google connected accounts
+must decrypt, sync, and send/receive as expected before production.
+
+**Gate:** no server/worker decryption errors; Outlook ingest and native connected-account sync both
+work on staging.
+
+## 8. Phase H — Build, stage, and run upgrades
+
+**Step H1 — Build an auditable image.** Push the branch, let GitHub Actions build, and deploy staging
+from an explicit GHCR tag or digest. Do not use floating `latest` for staging validation.
+
+**Step H2 — Run Twenty native upgrade commands.** Verify the exact command runner in the pinned
+target, then run the official version upgrade path. Confirm the channel-object migration command
+exists before naming/running it; if `upgrade:2-8:drop-channel-standard-objects` is present, treat it
+as destructive and restore-only reversible.
+
+**Step H3 — Run POP upgrade commands, not ad-hoc raw SQL.** Convert or wrap POP migrations `001`-`012`
+as committed, idempotent upgrade steps. If a wrapper intentionally executes SQL files, the command
+must enforce ordering, idempotency, logging, and repeatability. Run POP upgrades twice on staging and
+verify the second run is a no-op.
+
+**Gate:** staging has passed native upgrades, POP upgrades, and the full Phase I checklist.
+
+## 9. Phase I — Verification checklist
+
+Run on staging, then repeat at production cutover.
+
+1. Server and worker containers are healthy, on the expected image digest, and free of decryption or
+   migration errors.
+2. Microsoft connected accounts decrypt; Outlook ingest imports a test email into custom
+   `emailMessage`.
+3. Gmail parser test preserves display names.
+4. Email router assigns a known customer-domain email to the expected company/department.
+5. Subdomain matching, ignore rules, PO/SO extraction, and thread/subject scans behave as before.
+6. Rerouter cron runs and can improve previously unrouted emails.
+7. Email-contact sync creates/updates Company and Person records with the expected domain rules.
+8. Routing views/nav are present: **Needs Routing**, **Unrouted Emails**, **Unrouted Notes**, plus
+   captured grouping/panel views from migrations `010`/`011`.
+9. POP UI works: Monday dashboard, required POP widgets/routes, relation picker behavior,
+   primary-buyer/company scoping, and `ParticipantChip`.
+10. Channel workspace objects are gone only after the native migration, and email/calendar sync still
+    works.
+11. The four rebuilt automations fire successfully.
+12. Re-running POP upgrade steps creates no duplicate folders/views/workflows.
+
+## 10. Phase J — Production cutover
+
+**Step J1 — Fresh prod backup and restore check.** Repeat Phase A immediately before cutover.
+
+**Step J2 — Confirm deployment topology.** Identify whether Coolify deploys split server/front/worker
+images or a single image:
+- If split, deploy server and worker first, run upgrade commands, then open/deploy front.
+- If single image, deploy once, immediately run upgrade commands, and keep user access gated until
+  Phase I passes.
+
+**Step J3 — Run upgrades in order.** Run Twenty native upgrades, then POP upgrade commands. Never run
+undocumented SQL by hand.
+
+**Step J4 — Repeat Phase I against `https://crm.designflow.app`.**
+
+**Rollback:** if any gate fails, redeploy the previous image digest through Coolify. If destructive
+migrations already ran, restore the fresh cutover dump:
+```bash
+docker exec -i twenty-postgres pg_restore -U twenty -d twenty --clean --if-exists \
+  < backups/twenty_preupgrade_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.dump
+```
+
+## 11. Phase K — Post-cutover and future fork reduction
+
+1. Fast-forward `main` to the verified re-fork branch and record the pinned upstream SHA as the new base.
+2. Monitor worker/server logs, Outlook ingest, routing, rerouting, and contact sync for 48 hours.
+3. Confirm no remaining production drift affects correctness.
+4. Evaluate fork reduction only after the v2.8 re-fork is stable: native Fireflies, native contact
+   creation, native Microsoft messaging, AI agents, native dashboards, and workflow tooling. None are
+   cutover blockers and none are assumed drop-in replacements for POP routing semantics.
*** End Patch

# Part 2 — Execution Plan (re-fork to verified v2.8 target)

> **Audience:** a developer who has *never* touched this codebase. Follow the phases in order.
> This is a business-system preservation plan first, not a generic upgrade. The core strategy is
> still re-fork-and-reapply, but the target release, workflow support, encryption model, and
> migration mechanics must be verified before production work begins.

## 0. Facts and non-negotiables

| Deployed image | `ghcr.io/u2giants/twenty:latest` |

| Repo / upstream | `github.com/u2giants/twenty` / `github.com/twentyhq/twenty` |
| Release pipeline | GitHub Actions -> GHCR image -> Coolify API deploy (**never** SSH, never `--no-verify`) |
| Current base / target | `v1.20.0` + custom commits -> **verified v2.8 release commit**, pinned by SHA |

**Business-critical behavior that must survive:**
- Outlook email ingestion (`outlook-ingest.cron.job`) and the custom `emailMessage` pipeline.
- `email-router`, `email-rerouter`, `so-extractor`, ignore rules, domain/subdomain matching, and PO/SO extraction.
- Daily `email-contact-sync` company/person creation and its shared routing-domain semantics.
- `clickup-sync`, Fireflies meeting-note routing, AI opportunity summary, and POP custom objects/fields.
- Routing views/nav, POP navigation, Monday dashboard, required POP widgets/routes, relation picker behavior, primary-buyer/company scoping, and `ParticipantChip`.

**Do not do:**
- Do not reapply footprint trimming or bulk package/core deletions.
- Do not hand-merge generated translation files; regenerate them.
- Do not delete the `POP Creations CRM` app row unless a code-backed replacement is already proven.
- Do not migrate the dead v1.20 page-layout rows with SQL; remount wanted dashboards in code or a verified v2.x app mechanism.
- Do not replace Outlook ingest or email-contact sync with native Twenty messaging/contact creation as a drop-in.
- Do not click together workflows in the UI as unreproducible workspace drift.

## 1. Phase A — Safety net and restore drill

**Step A1 — Full production database backup.**
```bash
STAMP="$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"
docker exec -t twenty-postgres pg_dump -U twenty -d twenty -Fc \
  -f "/tmp/twenty_preupgrade_${STAMP}.dump"
docker cp "twenty-postgres:/tmp/twenty_preupgrade_${STAMP}.dump" "./backups/"
pg_restore --list "./backups/twenty_preupgrade_${STAMP}.dump" >/tmp/twenty_restore_list.txt
```
**Verify:** dump exists, is > 0 bytes, `pg_restore --list` succeeds, and the dump is copied off the
VPS. Restore this dump into a staging database before any production cutover.

**Step A2 — Snapshot current runtime state.** Export Coolify env vars for app
`rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92`, record the running image digest and current git SHA, and screenshot the
sidebar folders/views. Save these artifacts outside the deployment host.

**Gate:** do not proceed until the dump has been restored into staging and the restored staging app
can boot on the current image.

## 2. Phase B — Verify and pin the upstream target

**Step B1 — Fetch and verify the exact target.**
```bash
git fetch upstream --tags --prune
git tag -l 'v2.8*' --sort=-v:refname
git rev-parse <verified-v2.8-tag-or-sha>
git log -1 --format='%H %cI %s' <verified-v2.8-tag-or-sha>
```
If no v2.8 tag or intended upstream SHA can be verified locally after fetch, **stop**. Do not branch
from a stale `upstream/main`, a missing tag, or an assumed release name.

**Step B2 — Create the re-fork branch from the pinned SHA.**
```bash
git switch -c v28-refork <PINNED_UPSTREAM_SHA>
git describe --tags --always
```
Do this in a separate clone or worktree if the server checkout blocks branch creation. Do not rewrite
`main` until production cutover is complete and verified.

**Gate:** record `<PINNED_UPSTREAM_SHA>` in this document before implementation begins.

## 3. Phase C — Preserve the POP business system

**Step C1 — Port the custom data model first.** Reapply all POP object/field definitions before
runtime services:
- The 8 POP custom objects from `modules/pop-creations/standard-objects/`.
- Custom fields on standard `company`, `person`, and `opportunity` objects.
- The matching `twenty-standard-application` field-metadata utilities, including the
  `pop-creations/compute-*` utilities.
- Keep `company.creditStatus` intentionally dropped unless that prior decision is reversed.

This is the biggest backend reapply surface. It is not contained inside the clean `pop-creations`
module, and v2.8 may have moved the standard-application metadata pipeline.

**Step C2 — Port the `pop-creations` backend module and adapt it.** Copy
`packages/twenty-server/src/modules/pop-creations/**`, then fix imports, module wiring, queue/job
registration, workspace manager usage, event listener decorators, and internal repository APIs
against the pinned v2.8 target. Treat "no Git conflicts" as only a copyability fact, not proof that
the code compiles or runs.

**Step C3 — Keep live cron jobs and router services.** Preserve and register:
- `outlook-ingest.cron.job`, `email-contact-sync.cron.job`, and `clickup-sync.cron.job`.
- `email-router.service`, `email-rerouter`, `so-extractor`, Fireflies routing, and opportunity summary.
- Gmail display-name preservation in the shared messaging parser files.

**Gate:** server builds, the POP module loads, and targeted tests or local harnesses cover custom
field metadata, Gmail display-name parsing, router domain/subdomain matching, and rerouter queries.

## 4. Phase D — Backend shared-file patches

## 5. Phase E — Frontend: required business UI vs optional polish

## 6. Phase F — Rebuild the four dead automations reproducibly

The four intended automations are pre-launch features that never worked in production. Rebuild them,
but only with a reproducible code/upgrade path.

Decision order:
1. Verify whether the pinned v2.8 SDK/app system supports defining full workflows in code. If it

3. If workflow seeding is not practical, reimplement them as corrected app/server logic functions

Do not use unreproducible UI-clicked workflows as the final implementation.

| Auto-create LAT | `opportunity` updated; stage changed into PROPOSAL/NEGOTIATION | Create related `licensorApprovalThread`. |
| LAT follow-up task | `licensorApprovalThread` updated into follow-up stage | Create related follow-up `task`. |
| New-program checklist | `opportunity` created | Create the six template tasks from the existing listener source. |
| Contact auto-scope | `person` created | Set scope from department/company presence, else ignored. |

**Gate:** on staging, fire all four triggers and verify the created/updated records. Keep old listener
source until the replacement passes, then delete or quarantine the dead code.

## 7. Phase G — Configuration and encryption

Do these in **Coolify** or the target runtime config, not in Git.

**Step G1 — Verify the target encryption model.** Before adding new secrets, inspect the pinned
target's config schema/docs and confirm whether the required variable is `ENCRYPTION_KEY`,
`APP_SECRET`, a versioned key envelope, or another key-rotation mechanism. Keep the existing
`APP_SECRET`; do not assume it is obsolete.

**Step G2 — Add only confirmed required env vars.** Add the target-required encryption value before
the first upgraded boot. Diff against the A2 env snapshot and confirm nothing else changed.

**Step G3 — Connected-account decryptability check.** On staging, Microsoft/Google connected accounts
must decrypt, sync, and send/receive as expected before production.

**Gate:** no server/worker decryption errors; Outlook ingest and native connected-account sync both
work on staging.

## 8. Phase H — Build, stage, and run upgrades

**Step H1 — Build an auditable image.** Push the branch, let GitHub Actions build, and deploy staging
from an explicit GHCR tag or digest. Do not use floating `latest` for staging validation.

**Step H2 — Run Twenty native upgrade commands.** Verify the exact command runner in the pinned
target, then run the official version upgrade path. Confirm the channel-object migration command
exists before naming/running it; if `upgrade:2-8:drop-channel-standard-objects` is present, treat it
as destructive and restore-only reversible.

**Step H3 — Run POP upgrade commands, not ad-hoc raw SQL.** Convert or wrap POP migrations `001`-`012`
as committed, idempotent upgrade steps. If a wrapper intentionally executes SQL files, the command
must enforce ordering, idempotency, logging, and repeatability. Run POP upgrades twice on staging and
verify the second run is a no-op.

**Gate:** staging has passed native upgrades, POP upgrades, and the full Phase I checklist.

## 9. Phase I — Verification checklist

Run on staging, then repeat at production cutover.

1. Server and worker containers are healthy, on the expected image digest, and free of decryption or
   migration errors.
2. Microsoft connected accounts decrypt; Outlook ingest imports a test email into custom
   `emailMessage`.
3. Gmail parser test preserves display names.
4. Email router assigns a known customer-domain email to the expected company/department.
5. Subdomain matching, ignore rules, PO/SO extraction, and thread/subject scans behave as before.
6. Rerouter cron runs and can improve previously unrouted emails.
7. Email-contact sync creates/updates Company and Person records with the expected domain rules.
8. Routing views/nav are present: **Needs Routing**, **Unrouted Emails**, **Unrouted Notes**, plus
   captured grouping/panel views from migrations `010`/`011`.
9. POP UI works: Monday dashboard, required POP widgets/routes, relation picker behavior,
   primary-buyer/company scoping, and `ParticipantChip`.
10. Channel workspace objects are gone only after the native migration, and email/calendar sync still
    works.
11. The four rebuilt automations fire successfully.
12. Re-running POP upgrade steps creates no duplicate folders/views/workflows.

## 10. Phase J — Production cutover

**Step J1 — Fresh prod backup and restore check.** Repeat Phase A immediately before cutover.

**Step J2 — Confirm deployment topology.** Identify whether Coolify deploys split server/front/worker
images or a single image:
- If split, deploy server and worker first, run upgrade commands, then open/deploy front.
- If single image, deploy once, immediately run upgrade commands, and keep user access gated until
  Phase I passes.

**Step J3 — Run upgrades in order.** Run Twenty native upgrades, then POP upgrade commands. Never run
undocumented SQL by hand.

**Step J4 — Repeat Phase I against `https://crm.designflow.app`.**

**Rollback:** if any gate fails, redeploy the previous image digest through Coolify. If destructive
migrations already ran, restore the fresh cutover dump:
```bash
docker exec -i twenty-postgres pg_restore -U twenty -d twenty --clean --if-exists \
  < backups/twenty_preupgrade_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.dump
```

## 11. Phase K — Post-cutover and future fork reduction

1. Fast-forward `main` to the verified re-fork branch and record the pinned upstream SHA as the new base.
2. Monitor worker/server logs, Outlook ingest, routing, rerouting, and contact sync for 48 hours.
3. Confirm no remaining production drift affects correctness.
4. Evaluate fork reduction only after the v2.8 re-fork is stable: native Fireflies, native contact
   creation, native Microsoft messaging, AI agents, native dashboards, and workflow tooling. None are
   cutover blockers and none are assumed drop-in replacements for POP routing semantics.

---

## 12. Production drift audit (2026-06-02)

**Bonus — migrating fixes a latent bug.** `program-stage-change` and `lat-stage-follow-up` both do
`const { stage: newStage, stage: previousStage } = properties;` — destructuring the **same** field
twice, so their `if (newStage === previousStage) return;` guard can never be false and the action
**likely never fires today**. A native Workflow "updated → field changed to X" trigger removes the
bug entirely.

**Caveat — workflows are workspace data.** A no-code Workflow lives in the DB (like views), so to
keep it reproducible on re-fork it should be **defined in an app manifest** (the `twenty-fireflies`
app ships workflow-builder actions this way) rather than clicked together in the UI. Net trade:
~4 custom backend listeners (incl. a bug) → declarative workflow definitions in the POP Creations
app manifest.

### Summary of the fork-reduction opportunity (corrected)
- **Deleted (unreferenced placeholder dirs, zero loss):** the stub service files under
  `logic-functions/{outlook-ingest,email-contact-sync,clickup-sync}/`. The **cron jobs of the same
  names are LIVE and were kept.**
- **Genuinely dead — rebuild as native Workflows (post-v2.8, manifest-defined):**
  `program-stage-change`, `lat-stage-follow-up`, `new-program-tasks`, `contact-auto-scope` — 4
  unregistered listeners → declarative workflows (fixes the latent bug too).
- **Live — keep:** `outlook-ingest` (email pipeline), `email-contact-sync`, `clickup-sync` (idle),
  `email-router` / `email-rerouter` / `so-extractor`, `fireflies-ingest`, custom objects + fields.
- **Future consolidation to evaluate at re-fork (not drop-in):** Outlook ingest → native Microsoft
  driver; contact creation → `contact-creation-manager`; Fireflies → `twenty-fireflies`; AI summary
  → AI agent action — each is coupled to the custom email object/router, so validate before adopting.

---

## 15. VERIFIED: which custom features are silently dead in production (2026-06-02)

Verified by (1) code mechanism and (2) live production data in `workspace_93r34ew9zc9644a9y5f1yeylz`.

**Root cause for the automations:** the four standalone listeners subscribe with
`@OnEvent('opportunity.updated', …)` and read a **single-record** payload
(`payload.recordId`, `payload.properties.stage`). But the platform emits **batched** events
(`computeEventName` → `'opportunity.updated'`, payload = `WorkspaceEventBatch{ events: [...] }`,
each event having `.recordId` and `.properties.{before, after}`). The correct pattern —
`@OnDatabaseBatchEvent('opportunity', UPDATED)` with `payload.events[]` — is what the working main
listener and all native listeners use. So the four custom listeners fire but read `undefined`
fields off the batch (the stage ones then double-destructure `properties.stage`), crash into their
own try/catch, and **no-op**.

| Custom feature | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| `outlook-ingest.cron.job` | ✅ **LIVE & CRITICAL** | 10,507/10,507 emails have `outlookMessageId`; latest `receivedAt` = today — the whole email pipeline |
| `email-router` / `email-rerouter` | ✅ **WORKING** | 7,293 routed to a company (`COMPANY_ONLY`); 2,621 UNROUTED, 593 SKIPPED; `_emailMessage_reroute_backup` present |
| `email-contact-sync.cron.job` | ✅ **LIVE** | companies/people created from email domains (mostly since re-classified off `UNASSIGNED`) |
| `clickup-sync.cron.job` | ✅ **LIVE (idle)** | real ClickUp sync, but **0 opportunities** exist so nothing to do |
| `new-program-tasks` (listener) | ❌ **DEAD** | listener module not registered + 0 opportunities; 0 template tasks |
| `contact-auto-scope` (listener) | ❌ **DEAD** | module not registered; **all 8,602** people have `scope = NULL` |
| `program-stage-change` (listener) | ❌ **DEAD** | module not registered + wrong payload shape; 0 `_licensorApprovalThread` rows |
| `lat-stage-follow-up` (listener) | ❌ **DEAD** | module not registered + wrong payload shape; no LATs exist |

**Corrected count: the 4 `@OnEvent` automation *listeners* are dead** (their modules are never
imported, so they're never instantiated; the two stage ones also misread the batch payload). The
three `cron.job` features (`outlook-ingest`, `email-contact-sync`, `clickup-sync`) are **LIVE** —
the only "stubs" were unused placeholder service files (now deleted). `clickup-sync` and the
opportunity-triggered automations also have **0 opportunities** to act on.

**Implication for the re-fork:** keep the live cron jobs (especially `outlook-ingest`). Re-implement
the 4 dead automations as native Workflows (§14c) — they will work *for the first time*.

**⚠️ Context — the system is PRE-LAUNCH, not live.** The empty production data (0 opportunities,
0 LATs, all people unscoped) reflects that the CRM has **not gone live yet** and not everything
specced is exercised — it is **not** evidence of abandonment. The opportunity / program / LAT
features and their four automations are **intended functionality**. So the decision is settled:
**rebuild all four as native Workflows** (do not delete). They were specced, were never wired up
correctly, and have simply never had data flow through them yet.

**Decision (in the plan): rebuild all 4 automations as native, manifest-defined Workflows at the
re-fork** — `program-stage-change`, `lat-stage-follow-up`, `new-program-tasks`, `contact-auto-scope`.
The native Workflow engine already exists (even in v1.20) and is the correct mechanism; the custom
`@OnEvent` listeners were a reinvention that never ran. See Part 2 · Phase E-bis below.
